title: Scaffold flow (authored)
description: A declarative wizard graph the IDE interprets. DELIBERATELY TINY: choices, equality guards, prereq/credential refs, command templates, branches. No loops, no expressions — if it needs those, it belongs in the engine, not the flow.

minEngineVersion.description: oldest IDE engine that can interpret this flow

id.description: action id. Known: create-new · open-existing · clone (generic git URL) · clone-github · clone-cloud (magento-cloud project:get) · clone-git

from.description: data ref for `choose`/`resolve`, e.g. registry.versions or registry.requirements(${ref})

allowCustom.description: for `choose`: let the user type a branch/ref

validate.description: engine-side check. Known: git-ls-remote (ref exists) · git-url (well-formed git URL) · empty-target (${parent}/${name} must NOT exist OR be empty) · url-host-detect (parse host+scheme to pick the credential)

needs.description: credential flags this step requires, collected JUST-IN-TIME via secure form (never chat), reused from the OS keychain. gitHostAuth is HOST-SCOPED (keyed by bitbucket.org / gitlab.com / self-hosted domain) and resolves to an SSH key (ssh URL) or a host token/PAT (private https).

command.description: parameterized template, ${field} interpolated. NEVER raw user/remote strings.

branches.description: for kind=branch: map of choice value -> steps[]
